title: Deep Dive into IA32 Protected Mode Architecture

resources.title: Intel IA32 architecture documentation protected mode

description: This step covers the advanced features of IA32's protected mode, focusing on privilege levels (rings), segment selectors, and descriptors. You will understand how the CPU enforces memory protection and access control, laying the groundwork for OS development and security analysis.

title: Advanced Memory Management: Segmentation and Paging

resources.title: IA32 segmentation and paging deep dive

description: Explore the sophisticated mechanisms of segmentation and paging, including Global Descriptor Tables (GDT), Local Descriptor Tables (LDT), and Page Tables. This section will clarify how virtual addresses are translated to physical addresses and how memory protection is implemented at a granular level.

title: Interrupts, Exceptions, and System Calls

resources.title: IA32 interrupt descriptor table system calls

description: Understand the full spectrum of interrupt handling (hardware and software) and exception processing in IA32, utilizing the Interrupt Descriptor Table (IDT). You will learn how the operating system intercepts events and manages transitions between user and kernel space through system calls.

title: Advanced Instruction Sets: SIMD, SSE, and AVX
description: Dive into Single Instruction, Multiple Data (SIMD) extensions like SSE and AVX, crucial for high-performance computing and multimedia processing. You will learn how to leverage these instructions to perform parallel operations on large data sets, significantly boosting application speed.

title: CPU Cache Coherency and Pipeline Optimization
description: Explore the complex world of CPU caches (L1, L2, L3) and their impact on performance. This step covers cache coherency protocols, branch prediction, and pipeline stalls, teaching you how to write cache-friendly assembly code for maximum efficiency.

title: IA32 Binary Exploitation Fundamentals
description: Understand common vulnerabilities in IA32 binaries, such as buffer overflows, format string bugs, and return-oriented programming (ROP). This module focuses on analyzing disassembled code to identify exploitable flaws and constructing proof-of-concept exploits.

title: Defensive Assembly and Exploit Mitigations

resources.title: Stack canaries and RELRO IA32 security

description: Learn about various exploit mitigation techniques like NX bit (DEP), ASLR, Stack Canaries, and RELRO, and how they challenge traditional exploitation methods. You will explore how these protections work at the assembly level and how to write more secure low-level code.

title: Linkers, Loaders, and Dynamic Linking in IA32

resources.title: IA32 dynamic linking PLT GOT explained
IA32 linker loader process deep dive

description: Investigate the process by which object files are combined into executables and loaded into memory. This includes understanding relocation tables, symbol resolution, and the mechanisms behind dynamic linking (PLT/GOT) and its implications for program execution and security.

title: Compiler Backend and IA32 ABI Analysis
description: Examine how high-level language constructs are translated into IA32 assembly by compilers, focusing on calling conventions (cdecl, stdcall, fastcall), stack frame management, and register usage. This knowledge is critical for reverse engineering and optimizing compiler output.

title: Concurrency Primitives and Multithreading in IA32
description: Explore the low-level implementation of concurrency primitives, including atomic operations (e.g., LOCK prefix, CMPXCHG), mutexes, and semaphores using IA32 instructions. This section prepares you to understand and implement synchronization mechanisms in multithreaded environments.

title: Virtualization Extensions (Intel VT-x) and Hypervisors
description: Gain insight into Intel's virtualization technology (VT-x) and its role in modern hypervisors. Understand how hardware-assisted virtualization allows multiple operating systems to run efficiently on a single machine, including concepts like VMX root/non-root operations.
